title: mentha longifolia subsp. erminea
intro: Mentha longifolia subsp. erminea, also known as Woolly Mint, is a subspecies of the Mint plant characterized by its woolly or hairy leaves. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oil, which contains menthone and menthol, known for their analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Woolly Mint include relief from headaches, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate sore muscles. Woolly Mint can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and topical creams, allowing for a range of uses in aromatherapy and traditional medicine.
